text.title: Writing Implementation Plans: Step-by-Step Workflow
text.summary: 8-phase procedure for creating implementation-plan.md from approved design.md, with the 100k rule, wave algorithm, and quality gate.

list.item: Read constitution.md — principles and tech stack standards
Read spec.md — extract all FR-X IDs to verify coverage
Read design.md — extract all AD-X and full file change table (this is the task input)
Review features/done/ — find pattern examples from completed implementations
Phase 2 (Complexity): for each file in design.md — estimate tokens (LOC + deps + business logic + tests); flag >60k single-file estimates for decomposition
Phase 3 (Work Units): group related files into logical work units (high cohesion, low coupling); apply 8-80 rule — not too many files, not too few

rule.statement: Every task must fit within 100k token context window: research ~20k + task file ~5k + implementation ~50k + buffer ~25k = total < 100k.
rule.rationale: Tasks exceeding 100k tokens force the executor to truncate context, producing incomplete or inconsistent implementations. Split on logical boundaries before writing the plan; splitting during execution is more costly.

list.item: If any work unit estimate > 75k: split into two tasks with a clear boundary
Boundary options: model vs service layer, happy-path vs error-handling, core logic vs tests
Map Finish-to-Start dependencies between tasks (most common: task B reads output file of task A)
Check implicit dependencies: shared files, module imports, schema order, API producer before consumer
Run tsort validation — no cycles allowed before wave analysis
Phase 6 (Draft): overview (total tasks, critical path, est. tokens), wave analysis table, per-task entries
Phase 7 (Review): call faion-sdd-reviewer-agent (mode: plan) — checks 100k compliance, dependency correctness, FR/AD coverage
Phase 8 (Save): write to .aidocs/features/{feature}/implementation-plan.md with status: Draft (human/reviewer sets to Approved)
